decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the limit (85.0°C). The previous attempt to increase spreader conductivity failed due to exceeding the upper bound of 500.0 W/(m*K). Since the material property is already at its maximum effective value, the next most impactful lever is to increase the physical size of the heat spreader to enhance lateral heat spreading. I will increase the spreader width by 20% (from 0.7 to 0.84), which is within the allowed 1.5x step ratio and fits within the design domain bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing the heat spreader width expands the area available for lateral heat conduction away from the chip, reducing thermal resistance and lowering the peak temperature without violating material bounds.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
Improved lateral heat distribution across the top layer
risk_notes: Widening the spreader brings it closer to the left/right domain boundaries; ensure it does not exceed the design domain width of 1.2m (current x0=0.25, new width 0.84 -> x1=1.09, which is safe).
If this geometric change is insufficient, subsequent steps may need to evaluate base plate conductivity or vertical positioning.
